summary: Performance refactor (self-hosted vendor assets + subsetted Bootstrap-Icons font) verified across 6 user-facing pages. All vendor assets return 200, ZERO external CDN/font requests, Inter/Manrope applied, Bootstrap JS dropdowns work, region-aware checkout regressions passed (US/CA/AU placeholders + country pre-select + currency labels). ONE broken icon found: bi-flask is referenced on /checkout.php (line 378), /order-view.php, /admin.php but was omitted from the 310-glyph subset, so it renders as an empty tofu box on the Stripe demo/test-mode trust badge in checkout.

ui_bugs.issue: bi-flask glyph is missing from the subsetted bootstrap-icons-subset.woff2 (subset has 310 of 311 used icons). Renders as empty box on /checkout.php (line 378 trust strip). Also affects /order-view.php and /admin.php (TEST mode pill, sandbox key banners).

issues: Login page body computed font-family is -apple-system/BlinkMacSystemFont/Segoe UI (system stack), NOT Inter/Manrope. login.php loads bootstrap.min.css + bootstrap-icons.min.css from /assets/vendor but does NOT include fonts.css (Inter/Manrope @font-face). Likely intentional for the standalone admin login page (same pattern as iteration_25), but worth confirming with E1.

verified_features: All 7 vendor assets return 200: bootstrap.min.css (227KB), bootstrap.bundle.min.js (79KB), bootstrap-icons.min.css (12KB), fonts.css (598B), Inter.woff2 (47KB), Manrope.woff2 (24KB), bootstrap-icons-subset.woff2 (22.4KB).
ZERO external requests to cdn.jsdelivr.net, fonts.googleapis.com, fonts.gstatic.com on home, /shop.php, product, /subscriptions.php, /contact.php, /login.php.
Inter, Manrope computed font-family present on document.body for home/shop/product/subscriptions/contact (not Times fallback).
Bootstrap JS dropdown verified working: currency selector (data-testid=currency-selector) opens .dropdown-menu.show showing AU/CA/EU/UK/US options. Nav dropdowns also open.

Region-aware checkout (regression OK): /checkout.php -> country=US, #co-zip placeholder=90210; /ca/checkout.php -> country=CA, placeholder=K1A 0B1; /au/checkout.php -> country=AU, placeholder=2000. Add-to-cart via /ajax/cart.php returned {ok:true,count:1}.
No missing icon glyphs detected on /, /shop.php, /product.php?slug=windows-11-pro, /subscriptions.php, /contact.php, /login.php (computed ::before content is non-empty for every i.bi element).
action_items: Add bi-flask to the subset whitelist that builds /assets/vendor/fonts/bootstrap-icons-subset.woff2 and bootstrap-icons.min.css, then re-subset. Codebase scan (grep -rohE 'bi-[a-z0-9-]+' --include='*.php') confirms bi-flask is the ONLY currently-used icon missing from the subset (310 in subset vs 311 actually referenced).
(Optional) If admin login should match the rest of the site visually, add fonts.css to login.php so the body picks up Inter/Manrope. Currently it falls back to the system font stack.

(Optional hardening) The subset build appears to use a static whitelist. Several admin pages emit dynamic icon classes (e.g. `bi bi-<?= $icon ?>`, `bi bi-<?= $o==='Mac'?'apple':($o==='Windows'?'windows':'pc-display') ?>`). These are stored in the safety set today, but flag for future refactors so dynamic glyphs don't regress.
critical_code_review_comments: Subset build needs a guard: a CI grep for `bi-[a-z0-9-]+` across *.php should diff against the subset whitelist and fail the build if any token is missing. bi-flask slipped through.
login.php duplicates header markup but skips fonts.css — consider sharing the asset block via an include so future vendor changes propagate automatically.
Bootstrap-icons.min.css size is 12.7KB and points to fonts/bootstrap-icons-subset.woff2 — good. fonts.css is only 598B with two @font-face declarations — fine.

success_rate.frontend: 92% (1 broken icon out of 311 used; all other checks pass)

seed_data_creation: Added 1 item to cart via /ajax/cart.php action=add (slug=microsoft-office-2024-professional-plus-windows) to exercise checkout. Cart is session-based so it does not persist across browser contexts.
context_for_next_testing_agent: After E1 adds bi-flask to the subset, re-fetch /assets/vendor/bootstrap-icons.min.css (should contain '.bi-flask::before') and /assets/vendor/fonts/bootstrap-icons-subset.woff2 (size will grow slightly from 22.4KB), then load /checkout.php in a browser and confirm the test-mode badge shows the flask glyph (not a box). The detection script used: iterate i.bi/span.bi elements and check getComputedStyle(el,'::before').content is not 'none'/empty. Region-aware checkout + currency labels are already passing — no need to re-verify unless header/footer changes again.
rca of the issue: Bootstrap-Icons font was subsetted from 130KB (2050 glyphs) to 23KB (310 glyphs) by hand-picking from a codebase scan + safety set. The scan missed bi-flask which appears in 4 templates: checkout.php (Stripe demo badge), order-view.php (TEST pill), admin.php twice (sandbox warning + keys card). Reproduction: open /checkout.php in the preview URL, scroll to the trust strip; an empty rectangle appears where the flask icon should be. Mitigation: add 'bi-flask' to the subset glyph list and rebuild the woff2 + CSS. Long-term: add a CI step that diffs `grep -rohE 'bi-[a-z0-9-]+' --include='*.php'` against the subset whitelist.
